title: Fix MongoDB Projection to Handle Nested Fields with Missing Keys

statement.markdown: You are given a MongoDB document and a projection object. The projection object has nested fields with missing keys. Your task is to fix the buggy code that projects the MongoDB document according to the projection object, handling missing keys correctly.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a MongoDB document (JSON object) and a projection object (JSON object). The projection object may have nested fields with missing keys.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be the projected MongoDB document (JSON object).
statement.notes: You should handle missing keys in the projection object correctly, i.e., ignore them and not throw an error.

constraints.description: The number of fields in the MongoDB document
The number of fields in the projection object

examples.explanation: The projection object specifies that only the 'name' and 'address.street' fields should be included. The 'age' field should be excluded.
The projection object specifies that only the 'age' field should be included. The 'address' field and its nested fields should be excluded.

hints.text: Consider using the `$` operator to handle nested fields in the projection object
